meta.title: FlowMind Schema Runtime Cutover
meta.description: Decision brief for hard-cutting executable FlowMind authoring to graph v1 while folding app-runtime semantics into Lev fractal roots, capabilities, and proof gates.

props.title: FlowMind Schema Runtime Cutover
props.subtitle: One executable graph target, one proof spine, no split-brain app runtime.

props.content: Lev should hard-cut new executable FlowMind authoring to `flowmind.graph.v1` / GraphDocument. Historical flow-like files stay discoverable, but they must be classified before they can be selected or executed.

props.label: RECOMMENDATION
props.title: The Cutover Decision
props.subtitle: This is a runtime convergence move, not a new manifest system.

props.title: Chosen path
props.content: New executable flows target graph v1. Legacy `.flow.yaml` files become classified compatibility artifacts. App-runtime semantics become facets of existing fractal roots, Poly capabilities, FlowMind overlays, and RunEnvelope proof receipts.

new executable flow = flowmind.graph.v1 GraphDocument
legacy flow-like file = classified compatibility artifact
app-runtime semantics = fractal roots + capability cascade + FlowMind
proof control = RunEnvelope + receipts + evidence spine

props.label: EVIDENCE
props.title: What The Sampling Found
props.subtitle: The cutover is driven by concrete repository inventory, not taste.

props.label: MODEL
props.title: Runtime Control Plane
props.subtitle: Discovery, admission, execution, and proof stay separate.

props.caption: Flow declarations move through discovery, audit, resolution, compilation, execution, and receipts.
props.content:
flowchart TD
  A[Fractal root / config scope] --> B[Poly discovers cards, capabilities, flow declarations]
  B --> C[FlowMind declaration auditor]
  C --> D{Executable and proof-bearing?}
  D -->|yes| E[FlowMind resolution cascade]
  D -->|no| X[Typed diagnostic and remediation]
  E --> F[Compile graph + overlays]
  F --> G[Orchestration RunEnvelope]
  G --> H[Harness provider/session binding]
  H --> I[Receipts + gate proofs + audit report]

props.title: 1. Discover
props.content: Poly indexes flow paths and capability declarations. It does not interpret FlowMind semantics.

props.title: 2. Audit
props.content: FlowMind classifies parser status, exec validity, proof-bearing status, selectability, and remediation.

props.title: 3. Compile
props.content: Only admitted graph v1 declarations compile into GraphDocument plus declared overlays.

props.title: 4. Execute
props.content: Orchestration owns ACT/VERIFY, RunEnvelope, scheduling, and proof lifecycle.

props.title: 5. Prove
props.content: Harness bindings, receipts, and gate proofs produce the durable evidence trail.

props.label: BOUNDARIES
props.title: Owner Matrix
props.subtitle: The cutover works only if each layer keeps its job.

props.label: MIGRATION
props.title: Compatibility Classes
props.subtitle: Classification makes old files visible without giving them accidental runtime authority.

props.label: OVERRIDES
props.title: Programmable, But Not Proofless
props.subtitle: Project and user customization are allowed; silent proof weakening is not.

props.content:
Config policy defaults:
  XDG/user < workspace/project < entity-local < explicit run flag

Flow selection:
  entity-local .flow.yaml -> .lev/flows/*.flow.yaml -> plugins/*/flows/*.flow.yaml -> system default

Core proof:
  inherited unless override declares scope, reason, owner, evidence, and receipt

props.title: Fail-closed rule
props.content: A project can override the selected core loop, but it cannot silently remove ACT -> VERIFY proof gates, Intent Conservation, receipts, or evidence requirements. Weakening requires an explicit policy record and receipt.

props.label: PROOF
props.title: Pentagon / UltraQA Strategy
props.subtitle: The proof target is cross-boundary behavior, not just schema parsing.

props.title: Highest-risk claim
props.content: A file can parse or be discovered but still must not be selectable, executable, or reported as proof-bearing unless it satisfies graph v1 and the core proof contract.

items.title: Declaration auditor
items.description: Implement classes, report fields, fail-closed selectability, and fixture corpus.

items.title: Docs and DNA reconciliation
items.description: Update FlowMind graph v1 authority, stale v2 docs, owner matrix, and compatibility class language.

items.title: Config cascade and proof weakening diagnostics
items.description: Show user/project/entity/run resolution and require explicit evidence for proof weakening.

items.title: Overlay extension validation
items.description: Typed inject-before/after contracts plus invalid overlay fixtures.

items.title: Community examples and QA matrix
items.description: Upgrade existing mini-app examples and run Pentagon/UltraQA scenarios across prompt, FlowMind, and SDLC overlays.

props.label: SAMPLE
props.title: 176 flow files
props.content: Repo-wide sampling found 176 `.flow.yaml` files across graph, session, template, single-node, node-array, and custom historical shapes.

props.label: TARGET
props.title: 114 graph-shaped
props.content: Graph documents are already the dominant executable shape, which makes `flowmind.graph.v1` the right hard-cutover target for new authoring.

props.label: COMPATIBILITY
props.title: 20 session-compatible
props.content: Legacy session-step flows stay available only through an explicit compatibility route. They should not become the default authoring target.

props.title: False-green risk
props.content: 42 unsupported execution formats, 117 missing schema-version warnings, and 88 pre-verify-gate warnings prove parser acceptance cannot equal proof-bearing readiness.

props.label: ADMISSION
props.title: Admission Path
props.subtitle: Each step has one owner and one proof obligation.

props.label: CONFIG
props.title: Fractal scope
props.content:
**Owns:** fractal roots, policy defaults, XDG/project/entity/run cascade, canonical write target.

**Must not own:** flow compilation or runtime execution.

**Proof:** resolved scope report.

props.label: POLY
props.title: Discovery and projection
props.content:
**Owns:** Card, capability, operation, and flow declaration discovery/projection.

**Must not own:** parsing, compiling, or executing flow YAML.

**Proof:** discovery-only invariant.

props.label: FLOWMIND
props.title: Compile and admit
props.content:
**Owns:** resolution, schema validation, overlays, evaluator contracts, compilation, admission.

**Must not own:** worker pools, retries, or DAG scheduling internals.

**Proof:** auditor + graph admission.

props.label: ORCHESTRATION
props.title: Run lifecycle
props.content:
**Owns:** RunEnvelope, execution contract, ACT/VERIFY lifecycle, receipts, proof objects.

**Must not own:** fractal root config or Poly projection.

**Proof:** GateProof / receipt.

props.label: HARNESS
props.title: Provider binding
props.content:
**Owns:** provider sessions, tmux/resource handles, transcript/resource bindings.

**Must not own:** flow selection authority.

**Proof:** session binding receipt.

props.label: PLUGINS
props.title: Capabilities and overlays
props.content:
**Owns:** capability providers, local overlays, owner-local tests, example flows.

**Must not own:** a parallel proof or ontology system.

**Proof:** owner-local fixtures.

props.label: DEFAULT SELECTABLE
props.title: graph_v1_executable
props.content: GraphDocument with schema version and proof-bearing path. Admit through the normal FlowMind compile path.

props.label: COMPAT ONLY
props.title: session_compat
props.content: Legacy session-step flow accepted through an explicit compatibility route. Never default-selectable.

props.label: NON-EXEC
props.title: parser_compat_nonexec
props.content: Parser can read it, but the execution contract cannot prove it. Migrate or mark as metadata.

props.label: METADATA
props.title: system_native_metadata
props.content: System policy or metadata shaped like a flow file. Keep visible, do not execute.

props.label: SCHEMA
props.title: entity_schema_metadata
props.content: Entity schema declaration, not a runtime program. Consider rename or schema folder migration.

props.label: FAIL CLOSED
props.title: unsupported_custom
props.content: Custom historical shape with no exec contract. Emit owner/path/remediation diagnostics.

props.label: CORRECTNESS
props.title: Unsupported default selection
props.content:
Scenario: unsupported custom flow is selected as default.

Expected result: typed diagnostic and no execution.

Owner: `core/flowmind`.

props.label: BOUNDARY
props.title: Poly compilation attempt
props.content:
Scenario: Poly tries to parse or compile flow YAML.

Expected result: static invariant fails.

Owner: `core/poly`.

props.label: OVERRIDE SAFETY
props.title: Verify gate removal
props.content:
Scenario: project override removes the VERIFY gate.

Expected result: fail unless explicit weakening receipt exists.

Owner: `core/config` + `core/flowmind`.

props.label: OBSERVABILITY
props.title: Parser success misreported
props.content:
Scenario: parser success is reported as proof-bearing readiness.

Expected result: auditor separates parser, exec, proof, and selectability states.

Owner: `core/flowmind`.

props.label: MIGRATION
props.title: Legacy graph readiness claim
props.content:
Scenario: legacy session flow claims graph readiness.

Expected result: classify as `session_compat` only.

Owner: `core/flowmind`.

props.label: CAPABILITY
props.title: Missing provider
props.content:
Scenario: plugin capability provider is missing, disabled, or ambiguous.

Expected result: fail closed before dependent step body runs.

Owner: plugins + Poly.
